---
title: "distributions"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(R6)

base_dir <- "/projectnb/dietzelab/arober/pecan/modules/prob.tools"
code_dir <- file.path(base_dir, "R")
dist_dir <- file.path(code_dir, "distributions")

source(file.path(code_dir, "utils.r"))
source(file.path(dist_dir, "base_distribution.r"))
source(file.path(dist_dir, "normal.r"))
source(file.path(dist_dir, "multivariate_normal.r"))
```


```{r}
# Should throw error (add this as unit test)
abstract_dist <- Distribution$new()
```
      
```{r}
# Univariate normal.
x <- Normal$new()
x$print()

print("Samples:")
print(x$sample())
print(x$sample(flat=TRUE))
print(x$sample(5))
print(x$sample(5, flat=TRUE))

print("Log Density evaluations:")
samp_arr <- x$sample()
samp_flat <- x$sample(flat=TRUE)
print(x$log_density(samp_arr))
print(x$log_density(samp_flat))
```

```{r}
# Multivariate normal.
d <- 3
S <- matrix(rnorm(d*d), nrow=3, ncol=3)
C <- tcrossprod(S)

y <- MultivariateNormal$new(cov=C)
y$print()

print("Samples:")
print(y$sample())
print(y$sample(flat=TRUE))
print(y$sample(flat=TRUE, simplify=TRUE))
print(y$sample(4))

print("Log Density evaluations:")
samp_arr <- y$sample()
samp_arr_simple <- drop(samp_arr)
samp_mat <- y$sample(4)

print(y$log_density(samp_arr))
print(y$log_density(samp_arr_simple))
print(y$log_density(samp_mat))
```

```{r}
z <- ProductDistribution$new(x, y)
z$print(print_components=TRUE)
```
```{r}
# Sampling

samp_flat <- z$sample(3, flat=TRUE)
samp_arr <- z$sample(3, flat=FALSE)

print("Flat:")
print(samp_flat)

print("Array:")
print(samp_arr)

print("Flat to array:")
print(z$transform_to_array(samp_flat))

print("Array to flat:")
print(z$transform_to_flat(samp_arr))
```





Things to implement when defining a new distribution:
1. Inherit from `Distribution`.
2. Set the `.constraint` private variable.
3. Implement any relevant private methods for distribution functionality; most
commonly will be `.sample()` and `.log_density()`.
4. Implement `.validate_dist_params()` (currently this is not forced, but could be).
